frameworks.name: normative perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: tying subjective confidence directly to internal signal quality

frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: current estimate is biased toward the previous trial's stimulus

frameworks.name: metacognition / subjective confidence
frameworks.one_line: confidence as a driver of trial-to-trial perceptual bias independent of signal

core_question: Does subjective confidence, dissociated from objective performance, modulate serial dependence in visual estimation?
key_assumptions: confidence and signal quality can be experimentally dissociated
serial dependence indexes carry-over from prior trials
manipulations: stimuli engineered to dissociate task performance from confidence
trial-level confidence reports on a visual orientation estimation task
key_findings: high-confidence decisions produced stronger biases on the following trial
the confidence boost of serial dependence persisted when confidence was dissociated from performance
interpretations: subjective confidence, independent of signal quality, amplifies serial dependence
challenges normative accounts that conflate confidence with internal signal quality
